summary: Comprehensive testing of FixMyCar backend + frontend. Backend: 13/13 pytest tests passing (root, bookings create/list/validation, contacts create/list/validation). Frontend: home hero, floating pill nav (site-header + nav CTAs), stats cards, /book form with all inputs, booking submit flow (POST /api/bookings + booking-success + WhatsApp URL via window.open), /services renders all 10 categories with exact sub-service counts (emergency 9, mechanical 12, electrical 10, brakes 5, ac 5, tyres 7, maintenance 8, body-shop 9, cleaning 8, others 8), service detail (/services/battery-jump-start), /sos with sos-big-call/sos-call-btn/sos-whatsapp-btn, /gallery with filters + lightbox, /about, /contact form submit (200 + Message received!), and mobile viewport shows mobile-bottom-nav + fab-whatsapp + fab-sos + nav-mobile-toggle. All flows work end to end without console errors.

minor.issue: Missing required fields return 422 (FastAPI/Pydantic default) instead of 400 promised by handler code — the explicit 400 branch only triggers if fields are present but empty strings. Not a bug, but response code inconsistent for the 'missing' case. Frontend does not depend on this.

issues: Headline reads 'Professional Car Repair & Roadside Assistance at Your Doorstep.' — spec used 'At Your Doorstep' (capital A). Cosmetic only.

Section labelled with word 'Timeline' not found via text search (Mission/Vision/Why sections are present). May be present as a visual timeline without literal 'Timeline' text — verify if the label matters.

action_items: (Optional) Align validation response code: return 400 (as handler does) instead of relying on 422 from Pydantic when name/mobile/message missing. Could enforce with a validator or make fields Optional and validate manually — current UX is fine because frontend validates before submit.
(Optional) Confirm About page 'Timeline' section — if a labelled timeline is expected, add a heading with the word 'Timeline' for content clarity.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend: BookingCreate/ContactCreate use `str` (required) for name/mobile — Pydantic returns 422 for missing keys; the manual `raise HTTPException(400)` is only reached for empty strings. This works but produces two different error shapes.

Backend: list endpoints project out `_id` correctly (good). created_at is stored as isoformat string and rehydrated to datetime on GET — consider storing as native BSON datetime for query flexibility.

Frontend: BookingForm swallows POST failures silently (only console.warn) and still shows success + WhatsApp redirect. This is intentional per PRD (non-blocking) but user has no indication the booking wasn't saved. Consider surfacing a toast on failure.
Frontend: input-photos captures files locally but they are never uploaded — expected per PRD (user shares on WhatsApp). Confirm this is what the product wants long-term.
General: No admin panel (per PRD) — bookings/contacts can only be inspected via direct DB or GET endpoints (unauth). If desired, add basic API key auth on GET endpoints.
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Test bookings and contacts created with TEST_ prefix (TEST_Rahul, TEST_Priya, TEST_AutoBooker bookings; TEST_Contact, TEST_ContactUI contacts). Safe to delete or ignore.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend endpoints and frontend flows are verified. Test file /app/backend/tests/test_fixmycar_api.py is reusable as regression suite. If retesting: run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_fixmycar_api.py -v` for backend. Note validation returns 422 (not 400) for missing fields — tests accept either. window.open in BookingForm is called via setTimeout(600ms) after setSuccess — stub window.open on init script if needed and wait for [data-testid='booking-success'].
